title: Queues, Streams, Super Streams, Retention, and Replay · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers queues, streams, super streams, retention, and replay into a new queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.
Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Queue Stream Semantics
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 1052 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Queue Stream Semantics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Queue Stream Semantics, keep Queue Stream Semantics connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A lab messaging team handles case 1052 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 6198 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Workload Selection Evidence.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply Workload Selection Evidence without its stated preconditions, omit the queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Workload Selection Evidence for queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Workload Selection Evidence condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Workload Selection Evidence output and hide the violated queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Workload Selection Evidence.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.
